pi_items.source: Analyzed setup_task.sh seed data (lines 71-102) which defines exactly 5 rooms
setup_task.sh line 76 defines GroundTruth as 'Occupied' for Office with User:John Smith. Verifier.py line 47-51 expects 'Occupied'. Matches occupancy rule: Office+User→Occupied
setup_task.sh line 82 defines GroundTruth as 'Vacant' for Office with User:None. Verifier.py line 55-59 expects 'Vacant'. Matches occupancy rule: Office+NoUser→Vacant
setup_task.sh line 88 defines GroundTruth as 'Common Area' for Meeting Room. Verifier.py line 63-67 expects 'Common Area'. Matches occupancy rule: Meeting Room→Common Area

setup_task.sh line 94 defines GroundTruth as 'Common Area' for Storage (ignoring assigned user B. Custodian). Verifier.py line 71-76 expects 'Common Area' and notes this as a trick question testing rule application. Matches occupancy rule: Storage→Common Area (user ignored)

setup_task.sh line 100 defines GroundTruth as 'Occupied' for Office with User:Alice Doe. Verifier.py line 82-86 expects 'Occupied' and marks as preservation check. Matches occupancy rule: Office+User→Occupied

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims verified. The task uses 5 synthetic rooms (RM-A-101 through RM-A-105) created programmatically in OpenMaint via CMDBuild API. Expected occupancy states are: RM-A-101=Occupied (Office with assigned user John Smith), RM-A-102=Vacant (Office with no assigned user), RM-A-103=Common Area (Meeting Room), RM-A-104=Common Area (Storage despite assigned user B. Custodian), RM-A-105=Occupied (Office with assigned user Alice Doe). All states comply with documented occupancy rules: Office+User→Occupied, Office+NoUser→Vacant, Non-office→Common Area. Verifier validates these exact states with scoring: 20 pts each for rooms 101-103, 25 pts for room 104 (trick question testing rule comprehension), 15 pts for room 105 (preservation check). Pass threshold is 60/100.
